Metadata.title: Cordée.IA — Bootstrap a Claude Code project in 2 minutes
Metadata.description: Open-source generator for Claude Code + Claude Design starters. 9 questions, 14 stacks, stack-aware agents. MIT, free.
Metadata.ogDescription: Open-source generator. Answer 9 questions, download a ready-to-use .zip.

Hero.subtitle: Answer 9 questions. Download a ready-to-use .zip: stack-aware agents, skills, MCPs, design system, security hooks. No account, no paywall, no storage.

Method.intro: From the first question to the first useful prompt in Claude Code. No account, no CLI to install, no tracking. Generation runs server-side on Next.js, in memory, per request.

phases.title: Answer the 9 questions
phases.desc: Name, mode (greenfield or overlay), stack, agents, skills, MCPs, design system, extras, license. Go back at any point — nothing is locked.

phases.title: The generator customizes
phases.desc: The server reads templates, filters checked agents/skills/MCPs, substitutes your project name, description, colors, license. No file you didn't ask for.

phases.title: You download the zip
phases.desc: The .zip is generated on the fly and streamed. Nothing is stored server-side, nothing is sent to a third party (no Anthropic, no OpenAI). The form payload lives only for the HTTP request.

phases.title: You unzip
phases.desc: Greenfield: unzip into an empty folder. Overlay: run install.sh inside an existing repo, it backs up your prior .claude/ and drops the Claude config on top without breaking anything.

phases.title: You run claude
phases.desc: Greenfield: /kickoff initializes the project from scratch. Overlay: /audit maps the existing codebase in 10 minutes. Off you go.

Expertise.intro: Everything in the zip is readable, editable, deletable. No binaries, no lock-in, no Cordée.IA dependency once generated. It's your repo.

items.d: 10 agents available. test-writer ships with pytest for FastAPI, testify for Go, Vitest for Next.js. Not a generic template you have to rewrite by hand.
8 reusable skills. /kickoff, /audit, /design-handoff, /release, /standup… Invoke them like slash commands inside Claude Code.
Per-stack coding rules, git workflow, security (secrets, GDPR, healthcare when applicable). Loaded by Claude Code on every session.
pre-edit-secrets blocks .env and credential edits. post-edit-format runs Prettier or Black depending on the file. Native Claude Code hooks.
12 MCP servers to choose from: Notion, Sentry, Figma, Playwright, GitHub, Linear, Vercel, Supabase, Stripe, Postgres, Slack, Context7. Checked = configured.
DESIGN.md (Cordée granite+copper example, blank 9-section template, or none), .editorconfig, Dockerfile, GitHub Actions CI, .vscode, MIT/Apache/AGPL license.

items.title: You start 5+ client projects per year
items.quote: "I copy-paste my CLAUDE.md from the previous project every single time."
points: Generate a customized starter for each engagement
Client name and brand colors substituted automatically
No drift between projects — the baseline stays consistent

items.title: You want a team standard
items.quote: "Everyone has their own version of CLAUDE.md, it's a mess."
points: Generate the team's official starter once
Push it to a GitHub template repo
Every new project inherits it automatically

items.title: You run workshops or courses
points: Hand out the same .zip to the whole class
Live demo of the Claude Design → Code chain becomes possible

Process.intro: Everything in the zip, in the order you'll meet it when you open the project. No Cordée.IA dependency after generation — delete or modify whatever you want.

deliverables.d: Claude Code instructions customized with your project name, stack, and description.
Permissions, hooks, env vars, teammateMode auto (compatible with Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.7).
The agents you checked. Per-stack variants for test-writer and dependency-updater.
The skills you checked (/kickoff, /audit, etc.). Editable markdown.
Coding standards, git workflow, security. Loaded on every session.
The MCP servers you checked, ready to use. OAuth or env-var auth depending on the server.
9-section design system (example, blank, or absent — your choice).
Dual-mode script: run it to drop the config on top of an existing repo (overlay mode).

UseCases.introPart1: Cordée.IA is free, open source, MIT. Here's who actually uses it and how. If your case isn't covered,
UseCases.footnotePart1: 3 additional cases (consulting engagement, hackathon, migration from Cursor) are detailed in

items.title: Bootstrap a project from scratch
items.desc: Answer 9 questions, download the .zip, run claude. 2 minutes instead of 2 hours of CLAUDE.md config.

items.title: Add Claude to an existing project
items.desc: Overlay mode: install.sh detects your stack (Next, Nest, FastAPI, Go, Rust), backs up your existing .claude/, drops the config without breaking anything.

items.title: Run a workshop or class
items.desc: Generate one starter for 30 students. Distribute the .zip via Moodle or shared drive. Everyone starts on the same footing.

items.title: Demo Claude Design in a talk
items.desc: DESIGN.md ready to upload to claude.ai/design. Handoff to Claude Code button. /design-handoff skill scaffolds the Next.js. Full chain in 5 min.

items.title: Standardize a team
items.desc: Generate your team's official starter once, push it to a GitHub template repo. Every new project inherits it automatically.

items.title: Fast onboarding on a legacy
items.desc: Drop the overlay, run /audit. Claude maps the codebase, lists 5 risky zones, 5 safe zones to start, suggests 3 safe PRs.

Faq.intro: Six questions that come up often. If yours isn't here, open an issue on the repo — we reply within 24h.

items.a: Yes. Code is MIT-licensed, the generator is open source, no paywall ever. The repo is public at github.com/jdvot/cordee-ia.

items.q: Is my data sent to a server?
items.a: Everything is generated server-side on Next.js, on the fly, in memory. Nothing is stored, nothing is sent to OpenAI or Anthropic. The form payload lives only for the HTTP request, the .zip is streamed directly to your browser.

items.q: Can I modify the zip afterwards?
items.a: Yes — it's your repo. Modify, delete, add, force-push. Cordée.IA has zero connection to the project after generation: no phone-home, no tracking, no auto-updates.

items.q: Why Claude Code and not Cursor or Cline?
items.a: The starter is tool-agnostic — the agents and skills work with any tool that reads .md files. But the .claude/ structure, hooks, and settings are optimized for Claude Code first.

The starter recommends Anthropic's 90/10 routing: 90% Sonnet 4.6 for mechanical work (research, doc, deps), 10% Opus 4.7 + xhigh effort for heavy reasoning (challenger, code review, db migrations). Documented in the generated CLAUDE.md.
We don't. The repo is OSS under MIT. It's an asset I use on my own Claude Code engagements and workshops, shared because it can save others time. Not a product to scale.

CTA.intro: No account. No paywall. No storage. The .zip is streamed straight to your browser. Close the tab right after — everything you need is in the file.

Footer.tagline: Open-source generator for Claude Code + Claude Design starters. MIT, free, no account.

HowItWorks.title: How it works
HowItWorks.subtitle: Four steps, in this order. No account, no paywall.

steps.title: Answer the 9 questions
steps.desc: Project name, mode, stack, agents, skills, MCPs, design system, extras, license. 2 minutes max.

steps.title: We customize the templates
steps.desc: The generator keeps only what you picked and personalizes CLAUDE.md, .mcp.json, .claude/. Per-stack variants for the agents.

steps.title: You download the zip
steps.desc: A .zip ready to unzip into your repo (greenfield) or on top of an existing one (overlay).

steps.title: You run claude and start coding
steps.desc: Suggested first prompt: /kickoff (greenfield) or /audit (overlay). Off you go.

WhatYouGet.title: What you get in the zip
WhatYouGet.subtitle: Everything is versionable, readable, editable. No binary files, no lock-in.

items.name: .claude/
items.desc: settings, agents, skills, hooks, rules — based on your choices.

items.name: CLAUDE.md
items.desc: Claude Code instructions customized with your stack.

items.name: README.md
items.desc: Project docs with your name, stack, and choices.

QuestionnaireSection.title: Configure your starter
QuestionnaireSection.subtitle: Nine steps. No account. Generation is server-side on Next.js, nothing is stored.

Faq.title: FAQ
Faq.subtitle: Six honest answers. If something's missing, open an issue on GitHub.

items.a: Yes. Code is MIT-licensed, the generator is open source, no paywall ever.

items.q: Is my data sent to a server?
items.a: Everything is generated server-side on Next.js, on the fly, in memory. Nothing is stored, nothing is sent to OpenAI or Anthropic. The form payload lives only for the HTTP request.

items.q: Can I modify the zip afterwards?
items.a: Yes, it's your repo. Modify, delete, add, force-push. Cordée.IA has no link to the project once generated.

items.q: Why Claude Code and not Cursor or Cline?
items.a: The starter is tool-agnostic — the agents and skills work with any tool that reads .md files. But the .claude/ structure and hooks are optimized for Claude Code.
We don't. The repo is OSS. A shared asset, not a product to scale.

items.q: What if I have an existing project?
items.a: Pick "Existing project" in the questionnaire. install.sh adds only the Claude files on top of your repo, without breaking anything. Automatic backup of any prior .claude/.

validation.projectNamePattern: Letters, digits, dot, dash, or underscore only.
validation.projectNameHint: Spaces and accents are auto-replaced (e.g. "My AI Project" → "my-ai-project").

step1.title: What's your project called?
step1.subtitle: Repo name, slug, or product name. You can change it later.
step1.descPlaceholder: 2 lines about what your project does.

step2.title: New project or existing repo?
step2.subtitle: Mode determines whether we scaffold everything, or just drop the Claude overlay on top of your repo.
step2.overlayDesc: We add only the Claude files on top of your repo. Your code is untouched.
step2.teamSetupDesc: Adds CONTRIBUTING.md, CODEOWNERS, and the GitHub PR/issue templates. Recommended if multiple developers will work on the repo.

step3.title: Tech stack?
step3.subtitle: Optional. Used to pre-fill coding conventions in CLAUDE.md and to pick agent variants (test-writer, dependency-updater).

step6.title: Which MCPs?
step6.subtitle: You can always add more later in .mcp.json.

step7.title: Starter DESIGN.md?
step7.subtitle: Design system is the first thing a design agent opens.
step7.useExampleDesc: Useful to see a complete DESIGN.md in action and adapt it.
step7.emptyDesc: The skeleton, no content. Fill it in with your design agent.
step7.skipDesc: If your project has no UI.

step8.title: Extra files
step8.subtitle: Optional. You can always add them by hand later.

step9.title: Which license?
step9.subtitle: The LICENSE file is added to the project root. None = private code, all rights reserved.

agent_options.challenger: Devil's advocate, challenges every decision (Opus xhigh)
agent_options.pr-reviewer: PR review: diff, tests, breaking changes (Opus xhigh)
agent_options.page-writer: Owner of a Notion / markdown page (Sonnet)

extra_options.editorconfig: Shared indent + EOL + charset across IDEs
extra_options.makefile: dev / build / test / lint / clean targets
extra_options.vscode-settings: ESLint format on save, Tailwind IntelliSense

AGPL-3.0.label: AGPL 3.0
AGPL-3.0.hint: Strong copyleft. Any modified reuse must stay OSS.
